mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of embedded systems?
mcqs.explanation: Embedded systems are characterized by low power consumption due to battery-operated or power-constrained environments. Unlimited power supply is not a characteristic.

mcqs.question: The heart of an embedded system is:
mcqs.explanation: The processor (CPU/Microcontroller) is the central processing unit that executes instructions and controls all other components in an embedded system.

mcqs.question: A microcontroller differs from a microprocessor because it includes:

options.text: Memory and I/O on single chip

mcqs.correctAnswer: Memory and I/O on single chip
mcqs.explanation: A microcontroller is a complete system-on-chip with processor, memory (RAM/ROM), and I/O peripherals integrated on a single silicon die.

mcqs.question: Which memory type is typically used to store firmware in embedded systems?
mcqs.explanation: Non-volatile memory like ROM, Flash, or EEPROM is used to store firmware permanently as it retains data even when power is removed.

mcqs.question: An airbag control system in a car is an example of:
mcqs.explanation: Airbag deployment must occur within a strictly defined time limit. Missing the deadline (failure to deploy) can result in system failure/fatal consequences.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is an input device in embedded systems?
mcqs.explanation: Sensors are input devices that convert physical quantities (temperature, pressure, light) into electrical signals for the processor to interpret.

mcqs.question: The main advantage of SoC (System-on-Chip) in embedded systems is:
mcqs.explanation: SoC integrates multiple components (CPU, memory, I/O, radio) on a single chip, reducing PCB size, power consumption, and overall system cost.

mcqs.question: Which classification is based on performance and complexity of embedded systems?
mcqs.explanation: Embedded systems are classified as small-scale (8-bit), medium-scale (16-bit), and sophisticated (32-bit/64-bit) based on processing capability.

mcqs.question: Watchdog timer is used for:

options.text: System monitoring and recovery from failures

mcqs.correctAnswer: System monitoring and recovery from failures
mcqs.explanation: A watchdog timer monitors system operation and automatically resets the system if software becomes unresponsive, providing fault tolerance.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is a volatile memory?
mcqs.explanation: Random Access Memory (RAM) is volatile memory that loses its contents when power is removed. It is used for temporary data storage.

mcqs.question: Deterministic behavior in real-time systems means:
mcqs.explanation: Deterministic systems guarantee that tasks will complete within a predictable, bounded time period, essential for real-time constraints.

mcqs.question: A digital camera is an example of which type of embedded system?
mcqs.explanation: A digital camera operates as a standalone system performing dedicated functions (image capture, processing, storage) without external network connections.

mcqs.question: What is the primary purpose of an ADC in embedded systems?
mcqs.explanation: Analog-to-Digital Converter (ADC) samples analog signals from sensors and converts them to digital values that the processor can handle.

mcqs.question: Which bus is commonly used for connecting peripherals in microcontrollers?
mcqs.explanation: Microcontrollers use various communication buses: I2C (short-distance), SPI (high-speed), UART (serial communication) for peripheral connectivity.

mcqs.question: MTBF (Mean Time Between Failures) measures:
mcqs.explanation: MTBF is a reliability metric indicating the average time between system failures. Higher MTBF means more reliable system operation.

mcqs.question: The function of a DAC in embedded systems is to:
mcqs.explanation: Digital-to-Analog Converter (DAC) converts digital values from processor to analog signals for controlling analog devices like motors or audio output.

mcqs.question: Which type of embedded system requires guaranteed response within strict time limits?
mcqs.explanation: Real-time systems must complete tasks within specified time constraints. Missing deadlines can result in system failure or degraded performance.

mcqs.question: In embedded systems, what does 'firmware' refer to?
mcqs.explanation: Firmware is specialized software stored in non-volatile memory that provides low-level hardware control and is typically more permanent than application software.

mcqs.question: Which is NOT a type of memory used in embedded systems?
mcqs.explanation: Embedded systems typically do not use virtual memory due to resource constraints. They use physical memory (RAM, ROM, Flash, EEPROM) directly.

mcqs.question: Power consumption is critical in embedded systems primarily because:

options.text: Heat generation is the main concern
Many are battery-powered with limited energy

mcqs.correctAnswer: Many are battery-powered with limited energy
mcqs.explanation: Many embedded systems (IoT devices, wearables, remote sensors) operate on batteries where power conservation directly impacts operational lifetime.
